title: [Clefable] Battle Guide
summary: A [Fairy]-type physical [wall] whose [magic-guard] eliminates all indirect damage, letting it sit on the field through [stealth-rock], poison, and burn without lo[sing] HP.
strengths: [stealth-rock] utility in the same slot as the [wall] role
weaknesses: Speed 60 lets setup sweepers boost freely
Low offense fails to threaten bulky [wall]s

recommendedMoves.reason: Field utility that snowballs long-game value

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
[Clefable] is a pure-[Fairy] physical [wall] whose unique advantage is [magic-guard] — the ability negates all indirect damage sources including [stealth-rock], poison, burn, [leech-seed], and life-orb recoil. 95/73/90 bulk is only average, but the ability effectively raises its real staying power far beyond its stats, making it one of the most reliable long-game walls in the metagame.

## Strengths
[magic-guard] is the whole identity. [Clefable] never loses HP outside of direct attacks, so it can sit on the field indefinitely with [soft-boiled]. [moonblast] pressures [Dragon], [Dark], and [Fighting] switch-ins, [stealth-rock] turns every [wall] turn into a long-game advantage, and [knock-off] removes opposing items to widen the resource gap across the match.

## Weaknesses & Answers
[Poison] and [Steel] both hit for 2x and are common coverage types, so [Clefable] cannot switch into [gunk-shot] or [iron-head] freely. Speed 60 lets setup sweepers boost against it without pressure, which is where [knock-off] earns its slot — stripping an item often forces a switch. Low offense means [Clefable] cannot crack bulky walls, so the plan relies on attrition rather than breaking through.

## Recommended Moveset
[moonblast] is the locked STAB because [Clefable] still needs a threat move to force switches. [soft-boiled] is mandatory recovery — the whole long-game plan depends on it. [stealth-rock] is the utility slot that makes [Clefable] useful beyond walling, and [knock-off] handles item removal for long-game value. A [calm-mind] setup variant exists but sacrifices the utility slot.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[leftovers] is the default item because [magic-guard] stacks with the passive healing, effectively giving [Clefable] double-recovery. [sitrus-berry] is a mid-game heal alternative when the early turns are critical. Ability must be [magic-guard] — unaware is only a niche setup [counter]. Tera [Fairy] maxes STAB damage, Tera [Steel] flips the [Poison] and [Steel] weaknesses into resistances.

## Teammates & Synergy
[Clefable] wants [Ground] or [Fire] partners to cover the [Steel] and [Poison] weaknesses. Pair it with an offensive threat that can actually break walls — [Clefable] sets the board while the partner closes the match. Do not stack hazard removers on the team, because it denies [Clefable] [stealth-rock] value.

## Play Patterns
Lead or enter on turn two, drop [stealth-rock] on the free turn, then loop [soft-boiled] and [moonblast]. Use [knock-off] on setup attackers entering the field, and Tera on any telegraphed [Steel] or [Poison] move. The sweep win condition is attrition, not direct damage.
